title: Aktiechefen: "Tur att kriget kom i ett bra läge – ekonomin var stark"
summary_md:
# Aktiechefen: "Tur att kriget kom i ett bra läge – ekonomin var stark"

**Feed**: dagen-med-di  
**Published**: 2026-05-19  
**Market Sentiment**: Positive

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

Nordea's Nordic equity fund chief Marie Karlsson argues that 2026 began unusually strong — recovering global economy, falling rates, expansive fiscal policy and an accelerating AI investment theme — which has cushioned the market against the new war in Iran and resulting oil/commodity price spikes. She believes the economy can absorb a much higher oil price (real-terms normal up to ~$210) better than consensus fears, and sees Nordic AI/datacenter equipment names as attractively valued versus stretched US peers. Key positioning includes a large stake in renewable-diesel winner Neste and Norwegian oil producers where estimates have upside.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 NESTE — Neste | **BUY** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 80%

Renewable diesel producer using waste fats, not crude oil, but prices track diesel; major winner as oil/diesel prices rise; fund holds large position.

#### Reasoning

Neste produces renewable diesel from collected restaurant fats rather than crude, so its input costs are decoupled from oil while its selling price mirrors rising diesel prices — making it a structural winner in the current oil-spike environment. The host states the fund holds a very large position and the stock has already risen strongly this year, reflecting explicit conviction.

#### Sell Triggers

- if diesel/oil prices fall sharply
- if feedstock (waste fat) costs rise materially
- if renewable diesel margins compress

> *"Som heter Neste. De är världsledare på att tillverka och producera förnybar diesel... Så de är ju en jättestor vinnare i det här läget. Aktien har gått upp väldigt starkt i år men de har vi en väldigt stor position i."*

---

### 🟠 ABB — ABB | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Cited as a Nordic name giving AI/datacenter exposure; host argues Nordic valuations are less stretched than US peers, making the segment attractive.

#### Reasoning

Karlsson highlights ABB among Nordic equipment suppliers benefiting from genuine AI-driven capex ('real money' being invested in equipment that won't disappear). She notes Nordic AI-exposed valuations are far less stretched than US names, making the segment interesting, though momentum-driven and prone to sharp drops.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI capex investment cycle slows
- if valuations re-rate sharply on momentum reversal

> *"Det finns många bolag i Sverige, vi har ABB, vi har Atlas, vi har Munters, Alfa Laval."*

---

### 🟠 ATCO-A — Atlas Copco | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Named as a Nordic company offering AI-theme exposure; valuations seen as more attractive than US peers.

#### Reasoning

Atlas Copco is cited among Swedish industrials providing exposure to the accelerating AI/datacenter investment theme, where Karlsson sees real, durable capex. She finds the Nordic segment attractive because valuations have moved less than US equivalents, while cautioning the theme is momentum-driven.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI capex investment cycle slows
- if momentum reverses sharply

> *"vi har ABB, vi har Atlas, vi har Munters, Alfa Laval"*

---

### 🟠 MTRS — Munters | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Listed as a Swedish AI/datacenter equipment supplier; Nordic valuations seen as less stretched than US.

#### Reasoning

Munters is named among Nordic equipment names benefiting from genuine AI-driven installation capex. Karlsson argues Nordic AI-exposed equities are attractively valued relative to US peers and that the underlying equipment investment is durable, though she flags momentum risk.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI/datacenter capex slows
- if momentum-driven valuations reverse

> *"vi har ABB, vi har Atlas, vi har Munters, Alfa Laval"*

---

### 🟠 ALFA — Alfa Laval | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Cited as a Nordic equipment supplier benefiting from real AI-driven capex; valuations less stretched than US.

#### Reasoning

Alfa Laval is grouped with Nordic industrials supplying equipment for the AI investment wave, which Karlsson views as real and lasting capex. She considers the Nordic AI segment attractive due to comparatively modest valuations versus the US, while noting the theme can fall quickly.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI capex cycle decelerates
- if valuation momentum reverses

> *"vi har ABB, vi har Atlas, vi har Munters, Alfa Laval"*

---

### 🟠 NOKIA — Nokia | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Finnish company cited as offering interesting AI/data exposure in the Nordics at attractive valuations.

#### Reasoning

Nokia is named among Finnish companies giving Nordic exposure to the AI/data theme. Karlsson stresses the appeal of Nordic AI names whose valuations have moved less than US peers and where equipment investment represents durable 'real money'.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI/data investment slows
- if momentum reverses sharply

> *"Men vi har också bolag i Finland som Nokia, Wärtsilä."*

---

### 🟠 WRT1V — Wärtsilä | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Finnish company cited as an interesting Nordic AI-theme investment with less stretched valuations than US.

#### Reasoning

Wärtsilä is listed among Finnish names offering Nordic exposure to the AI investment theme. Karlsson argues the Nordic AI segment is attractive because valuations are comparatively modest versus US peers and the underlying capex is genuine and durable, though momentum risk remains.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI capex cycle slows
- if momentum-driven valuations reverse

> *"Men vi har också bolag i Finland som Nokia, Wärtsilä."*

---
